episodes.title: How can students cover Epstein connections on campus?
episodes.description:
The release of the Jeffrey Epstein files has raised questions that reach into institutions across the country, including college campuses. But how can student journalists effectively, responsibly and legally pursue those stories?

In this April 2026 webinar, the Student Press Law Center convened a powerful conversation with:
*️⃣  Julie K. Brown, Miami Herald reporter whose dogged reporting helped bring much of the Jeffrey Epstein story to light
*️⃣  Sterling Davies, managing editor of news at The Stanford Daily
*️⃣  Mike Hiestand, SPLC senior legal counsel
*️⃣  Aadi Kucheria, reporter at The Stanford Daily
*️⃣  Katelyn Polantz, CNN correspondent (moderator)
*️⃣  Nathaniel Rodwell-Simon, managing editor of The Chicago Maroon at the University of Chicago

Student journalists and educators: If you have legal questions or want pre-publication vetting of an important story, reach out to SPLC's free, confidential Legal Hotline: https://splc.org/legalhelp/

🔗  Find Julie K. Brown's reporting here: https://www.miamiherald.com/profile/218644665/
🔗  Find The Stanford Daily's Epstein reporting here: https://stanforddaily.com/?s=epstein
🔗  Find The Chicago Maroon's Epstein reporting here: https://chicagomaroon.com/tag/jeffrey-epstein/

Support SPLC's free legal support and resources with a donation today: https://splc.org/donate
